---
title: ""
toc: false
---

::: hero
![](images/logo.svg)

# GenoSuite

::: lead
Modern numerical-genomics analytics — a desktop application for multivariate
analysis of genetic marker data, in the spirit of NTSYSpc but rebuilt for the
genomic era.
:::

[Download](download.qmd){.btn-genosuite}
[User Manual](manual.qmd){.btn-genosuite}
[Source on GitHub](https://github.com/mqfarooqi1/GenoSuite){.btn-genosuite}
:::

## What it does

GenoSuite turns SNP / marker tables into publication-ready multivariate
analyses through a point-and-click interface — no coding required. It runs as a
self-contained Windows application: install one file and launch a graphical
workspace.

::: feature-grid
::: card
### Distance & clustering
Genetic distance matrices (Euclidean, Manhattan, correlation, Jaccard, Gower)
and dendrograms by UPGMA, Ward, and neighbour-joining.
:::
::: card
### Ordination
Interactive PCA and PCoA with scree plots, coloured by population.
:::
::: card
### Diversity & structure
Per-marker MAF, heterozygosity, PIC, and population differentiation (Nei's Fst).
:::
::: card
### GWAS
Single-marker association with PC structure correction; Manhattan and QQ plots.
:::
::: card
### Genomic prediction
Cross-validated GBLUP and machine-learning models (elastic net, random forest,
gradient boosting, stacked ensemble) with genomic breeding values.
:::
::: card
### Mantel test
Permutation-based correlation between two distance matrices.
:::
:::

## A look inside

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Population structure (PCA)](images/pca.png)

![Neighbour-joining tree](images/tree.png)

![GWAS Manhattan plot](images/manhattan.png)

![Genomic prediction accuracy](images/prediction.png)
:::

## Quick start

1. **[Download](download.qmd)** and install GenoSuite (or run it from source).
2. Launch it — a workspace opens in your browser.
3. On the **Data** tab, click **Load demo SNP dataset** (or upload your own CSV).
4. Explore the **Distance**, **Clustering**, **Ordination**, **Diversity**,
   **GWAS**, and **Prediction** tabs.

See the **[User Manual](manual.qmd)** for full details on every module and the
expected data format.

---

GenoSuite is free, open-source software released under the
[MIT License](license.qmd). © 2026 Muhammad Farooqi.
